raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I noticed an unauthorized charge for {$62.00} on a credit card ending in XXXX issued from Barclays. This was unsetteling for XXXX reasons - the first was I never activated the card, so no charges should have been allowed on it and the second was I didn't have a subscription to XXXX XXXX Live. I had cancelled it back in XXXX of XXXX. 

I immediately called Barclays to dispute this and gave them all the pertinent information. I received a letter from Barclays dated XX/XX/XXXX acknowleding the dispute and another letter dated XX/XX/XXXX stating they were awaiting a response from XXXX. 

In XXXX of XXXX the charge of {$62.00} was reversed and then put back on the card. I started getting charged interest, penalties and late fees for a fraudulent charge on an inactive card!! Very frustrating. 

I received a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX from Barclays stating that the credit limit on the same credit card was lowered to {$500.00} because it was delinquent. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I called XXXX several times and finally spoke to a XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX We spoke for XXXX XXXX  and they informed me they had no record of the credit card on file and that is was fraud. 

I reported the card as compromised to Barclays and then received a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX stating that the card ending in XXXX has been closed due to suspicious activity. 

I wrote a letter to Barclays with a copy to the CFPB on XX/XX/XXXX with a full explanation of what happened and Barclays refusal to fix the fraudulent charge on the card ending in XXXX. 

I then received another letter from Barclays dated XX/XX/XXXX stating that they would not credit my account because they were not provided with the true nature of the dispute when first notified of the claim. This is UNTRUE as I explained the nature in my calls and in my letter. 

I received a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX saying my account had been closed because it was past due. 

The Barclays case number for my complaint is XXXX XXXXXXXX.
